pages.raw_text:
Of local building trades councils
and that they hope the local al.
lance will be reorganized with
only unions who are affiliated with
the Interpational Structural Build
ing Trades alliance.


G., w. Miller, business agent of
the alliance, had not heard of the
Carpenters action this morning. He
refused to state what effect it
would have on the organization.


In case the alliance should dis
solve it will probably carry down
with it the board of business
agents. One of the greatest objec-
tons to the alliance that the car
penters had lay in the influence ex-
erted in that organization by the
business agents. The carpenters
claimed today that the alliance was
dominated by the business agents
to such an extent that it was no
longer a representative body. They
also stated that they would, as an
inidivual union, unite with other
unions in any movement calculated
to benefit organized labor.
